1.content:
I’m sure you’ve heard of companies offering money for people to participate in research projects; NASA offering $19000 to lie in bed for two months, medical companies paying people to test out their products, things of that sort.

Being 15 years old, I wouldn’t normally pay attention to things like that – at least until I learned of my mom’s financial troubles. My father had abandoned us years ago; leaving my mother (Abigail) to raise me and my twin sister (Kelsey) by herself.

Kelsey and I always assumed that everything was going fine; we had food on the table, clothes on our backs and a roof over our head. Mom made sure to keep her financial problems from us; choosing not to burden us with her problems. For my part, I was content to spend time with my best friends Amy Williams and Brian Sullivan.

You’re probably wondering why I’m telling you this. Well after learning of the severity of my mom’s financial issues, I decided look into participating in a research project – one that would, as it turns out, have a profound effect on my life.
1.title: Prologue

11.content:
My alarm woke me up just before 6:00, just as the sun was beginning to come through my bedroom window. I sat up in bed and stretched for a moment before getting to my feet. Walking out into the hall, I trudged quietly passed Kelsey’s room on my way to the bathroom.

After using the toilet, I got undressed and stepped into the shower; luckily closing the shower door just as Kelsey walked into the bathroom. Glaring at her through the shower door, I said, “Dammit Kelsey; you know better than to barge in when I’m in the shower.”

“Trust me; this is the last place I want to be right now, especially after last time.”

I blushed as I remembered an incident last week where Kelsey had the misfortune of seeing me naked with a little morning wood. While reaching for the shampoo, I said, “What are you doing in here then?”

I watched through the glass as Kelsey took a seat on the toilet, “I overheard Mom talking with Aunt Carrie last night; apparently
Mom was asking her $5000 to pay off some outstanding credit card debt.”

Opening the door just a crack, I said, “So Mom’s asking her sister for money; what’s the big deal?”

“This isn’t the first time I’ve heard Mom talking to Aunt Carrie about money problems; I guess things aren’t going very well for Mom financially.”

Looking away for a moment, I said, “I get where you’re going with this; but what can we do to help? We’re not old enough to have part time jobs yet.”

“I don’t know; I was hoping we could talk about it after school while Mom’s at work.”

Rinsing the shampoo from my hair, I said, “Sure, I don’t see why not.”
11.title: The Story Begins

111.content:
Kelsey and I decided to take advantage of the mild late-summer weather and walk to school; which was only 10 minutes away. As we stopped at the last cross walk before school, Kelsey turned to me and said, “It looks like you, and some of the guys in your history class, are in for some major disappointment.”

I thought that was a weird thing for Kelsey to say, especially out of the blue. Looking at her, I said, “What’s that supposed to mean?”

Handing me her phone, Kelsey said, “Take a look at this photo; Grace took it last night outside the movie theater.”

One of the 10th grade History teachers had gone out on maternity leave at the start of the school year; so, the school hired a long-term substitute to fill in. Ms. Harrison was in her mid-20s and fresh out of college. She was easily one of the most attractive women I’d ever seen; most of the students in my class, including some of the girls, were infatuated with her.

So you can probably imagine how painful it was to see a photo of her kissing Coach Jacobs, a gym teacher who was easily the most despised teacher in school. Handing the phone back to Kelsey in disgust, I said, “I can’t believe she’s seeing someone like him; What could she possibly in that bully?” groaning, I said, “That would be like if Fleur Delacour hooked up with Professor Snape.”

Placing her phone back her purse, Kelsey said, “Who knows; she could be into that sort of thing, or maybe he’s a different person outside of school.”

I found that to be highly doubtful, the guy was nothing but a bully; tormenting his students for the hell of it. As we started walking across the street, I said, “You know he’s the reason Skyler Graufman had to change schools, right? He loved picking on Skyler because of his weight.”

“Like I said, he could be a different person outside of school.” Looking at me as we started up the stairs, Kelsey said, “Besides; you’re living in a fantasy if you thought you ever had a chance with someone like her.”

“Gee, thanks for the vote of confidence.” Entering the school, I said, “You going to meet with your soccer buddies?”

“Yeah; we’re trying to decide to new lineup, now that Rachel got herself pregnant.” Sighing, Kelsey said, “I’ll see you and the others at lunch, okay?”

“Sure thing.”

Walking down the hall, I started thinking about Mom again; wishing there was something I could do to help her out. I wasn’t really watching where I was going as I headed towards my locker; rounding the corner, I had misfortune of walking right into Ms. Harrison as she left the History Department’s office.

I watched as the stack of papers in her hand fell to the ground; causing her to let out an annoyed sigh. Bending down to help, I said, “Sorry about that, Ms. Harrison; I guess I wasn’t really paying attention.”

Ms. Harrison looked at me briefly as we picked up the papers; for a moment I thought she was annoyed with me. “It’s okay, Kevin; these are only papers that took me most of the weekend to grade.”

It took us a few minutes to pick up the papers, and a few minutes more to sort them by class. Once that was finished, I said, “Sorry again, Ms. H; I’m just a little preoccupied at the moment.”

Looking at her watch, Ms. H said, “We’ve still got time before school starts; why don’t you come inside and talk to me.”

Realizing I didn’t have much time before my first class, I wondered if I should take Ms. Harrison up on her offer.
111.title: At School

1111.content:
Realizing she could write me a note to excuse my tardiness, I smiled and said, “Sure, if you don’t mind.”

Getting to her feet, Ms. Harrison said, “I wouldn’t have offered otherwise.” Gesturing to the office, she said, “If you wouldn’t mind getting the door for me, that would be great.”

Following her into the office, I took a seat opposite her desk while she sat on the edge. Briefly admiring how she looked in her skirt, I said, “This morning I learned my Mom’s been having some pretty serious financial problems. I’ve just been wondering if there was something that I could do to help her.”

Trying to sound sincere, Ms. Harrison said, “That’s not something you need to worry about, Kevin; especially at your age.”

“I know; but things haven’t been great for her since my Dad left.” Sighing, I said, “I want to help make things easier for her.”

Ms. Harrison was silent for a moment, probably thinking of how to respond. “If you’re really serious about wanting to help your mom; I might know how you could make a pretty decent amount of money.”

My interest was piqued, although I was apprehensive, “This isn’t something illegal, is it?”

“No, it’s not illegal; but, given your age, it will require parental consent.” Walking behind her desk, Ms. Harrison retrieved something from a desk drawer, “Have you ever heard of Radeon Scientific?”

“I think so; aren’t they the company that just opened a facility outside of town?”

Handing me a brochure, Ms. Harrison said, “They’re actually all over the country; they’re always looking for people to take part in long-term studies.” Sitting in front of me again. She said, “I’m actually taking part in a study right now; once it’s finished they’ll give me $75000”

Looking up from the brochure, I said, “What kind of study are you taking part in?”

“I’m really not at liberty to say, because it requires a certain amount of secrecy. But, I can tell you it’s changed me if ways I never thought possible.”

I know she said it required secrecy, but I couldn’t help but feel she was trying to draw me in. Looking at her, I said, “If it’s something embarrassing, I promise I won’t tell anyone.”

Ms. Harrison looked at me for a moment, as she carefully considered her next move. After a moment, she said, “I suppose there’s no harm in telling you; after all you’ve never given me a reason not to trust in you in all the years you’ve known me.”

I was confused now, since I’d only met Ms. H a few months earlier. “What are you talking about?”

Getting to her feet, I watched as Ms. Harrison placed both her hands on her hips; a move that always made it difficult for me and my classmates to concentrated. Looking at me, she said, “Let’s just say I’m no longer that chubby kid from gym class.”

I stared at her for a moment, not quite understanding what she’d just said. After a minute it finally hit me. In shock, I looked at her and said, “Skyler?”
1111.title: A Conversation with the Teacher

11111.content:
I was silent for a few seconds as I struggled to process what Ms. Harrison had just told me; in doing so, I looked at her in the hopes of finding something to prove her story. But there was nothing; everything about her screamed female, from her appearance to her personality. “I’m sorry; there’s no way you could be possibly be Skyler Graufman; he was this chubby kid and you’re, well...”

“What? Attractive? A woman?” looking at me, she said, “Trust me, Kevin; I’m telling you the truth. Who’d be crazy to make up something like this?”

“How is something like this possible?” trying to remain calm, I said, “Are you all woman?”

Sighing, as if annoyed by the question, Ms. H said, “I’m anatomically female if that’s what you’re asking.”

Reaching my left hand towards her chest. I said, “So those are real then?”

“They’re very real.” Slapping my hand away, she said, “I suggest you be more careful; unless you want be suspended for groping a teacher.”

Blushing in embarrassment, I said, “Sorry, I’m just having a tough time processing all this.” Sitting back in the chair, I said, “What would possess you to something so drastic?”

“More than anything, I suppose I did it because I was curious. I wanted to know what girls were like, and it wasn’t like they were lining up to date the old me. Now I’m getting firsthand experience about girls, and they’re even talking to me; they think I’m giving them advice when I’m really just trying to compare my experiences to theirs.” Looking at me, she said, “Once the study is finished, and everything’s undone, I’ll be $75000 richer and 30 pounds lighter.”

Ms. Harrison went on to explain how everything happened; from turning herself into a woman, to spending the summer in the tropics learning to act like one. Once she was finished, I said, “I have to ask; why come back here?”

“Everything about me was different; I guess I just wanted to be someplace familiar.” Looking at me, she said, “Besides, being back here has given me a real insight into some of your classmates.”

I took note of the fact that she’d said your instead of our when referring to my fellow students. Looking at her, I said, “What kind of insights?”

“Do you remember how you used to tell me people like Tommy Sinclair weren’t going anywhere because they’re idiots?” starting to gather things from her desk, Ms. Harrison said, “Well after grading a lot of their homework, I can say with confidence that you’re right. I hope they like living at home and flipping burgers, because that’s all they’ll be able to do.”

The bell rang just as I was about to ask one last question, “I’ve got to know; what’s it like being a teacher?”

“It’s actually quite rewarding; knowing that I’m molding young minds.” Smiling, she said, “There is just one thing I’m not a real fan of; something that a lot of my female colleagues sympathize with.”

I had a sinking suspicion that I knew exactly what she was going to say next, “Oh? What’s that?”

“I’m not fan of my students ogling me. Having once been a hormonal teenage boy, I can understand why it happens; but being the one who’s ogled is much different than being the one doing the ogling.” Sighing, she said, “I’m not singling you out, although I have noticed you tend to focus on my chest during class, but I wish you and your classmates would focus more on the lesson then the outfit I’m wearing.”

Getting to my feet, I said, “I can’t promise I’ll change overnight; but learning the truth about you is a big turn off.”

Writing a note for my teacher, Ms. Harrison said, “That reminds me; I’d really appreciate you not revealing anything about what we discussed.” Handing the note to me, she said, “If the truth ever got out, it would compromise the study.”

“Okay, Ms. Harrison; I promise not to tell anyone.”

Looking at me as we left the office, Ms. Harrison said, “Now don’t forget, you’ve got a test today; I hope you studied for it.”
11111.title: Ms. Harrison Comes Clean

111111.content:
I had trouble concentrating during my first two classes of the day; not because of the course material, but because of what Ms. Harrison had told me. I thought back on the last two months; seeing her chatting with other teachers, lecturing students for missing homework, etc. I even saw her once or twice buying feminine products at the store. To learn that she was once Skyler Graufman was a major shock.

I tried to keep from thinking about that as I walked into the cafeteria for lunch; quickly making my way over to my usual table. Kelsey was already there with Amy and Brian; the three of them chatting about something as I took my seat.

The four of us had been best friends since Kindergarten; well actually it started out as me and Brian, and Kelsey and Amy. Amy was on the soccer team with Kelsey, while Brian played lacrosse. In case you’re wondering, I was on the swim team.

I was unpacking my lunch when Brian turned to me, “Hey man; we were just talking about Ms. Harrison.”

I looked at Brian nervously; thinking they’d somehow heard about Ms. Harrison’s secret. Trying my best to remain calm, I said,
“Oh? What about her?”

“How’s she dating Coach Jacobs. With all the stories kids tell about him; I’m surprised she wants anything to do with him.”

I was relieved, for both her sake and mine, that Ms. Harrison’s secret was safe. But I couldn’t help but think to myself – You’d be even more surprised if you knew what I knew. Looking at Kevin as I drank my soda, I said, “Yeah; well Kelsey made a good point this morning, maybe he’s different outside of school.”

Looking at me, Amy said, “I doubt that’s the case; but who knows.”

Grabbing my sandwich, I turned my attention to Kelsey and said, “It’s funny that you guys were talking about Ms. Harrison; because she gave me some suggestions regarding Mom’s financial troubles.”

“Oh? Like what?”

“I can’t go into specifics right now; but if what she says is true, it’ll make us a lot of money.” Biting my sandwich, I said, “I was thinking about inviting her over for dinner tonight; to discuss everything with Mom.”

While Kelsey looked at me; Amy and Brian both started laughing. After a moment, Brian said, “Is that the only reason you want to invite her over, Kev?”

I knew what Brian was implying, but I wasn’t in the mood to play along; besides learning the truth about Ms. Harrison was a big
turn off. “Yeah man, that’s the only reason.”

**************************


After that the conversation turned to something more appropriate for the lunchroom, making plans to hangout over the weekend. With about 10 minutes left for lunch, I thought it would be a smart idea to speak with Ms. Harrison before class. Saying my goodbyes to the others, I packed my things and left the lunchroom.

Once my lunchbox was back in my locker, I walked down the hall to Ms. Harrison’s room. I got there just as a freshman girl walked out of the room, saying, “Thanks for the advice, Ms. H.”

Standing in the doorway, I saw Ms. Harrison sitting at her desk poking at what looked like a chicken salad. Without even thinking, I said, “Hey Skyler, you got a minute?”
111111.title: At Lunch

1111111.content:
She coughed, a smile piece of chicken falling from her mouth. Wiping her mouth, she glared at me, "What did you call me?"

I blinked, not sure what the problem was, “Uh…”

"I seem to recall asking you to keep that a secret, Mr. Scott.” Getting to her feet, she continued, “I should have known you’d have a problem with that; you never could keep your mouth shut. What if one of my colleagues had been in here? That happens from time to time, you know."

Surprised to find myself genuinely afraid, I said, “Sorry, I guess I wasn’t thinking.”

“Well, you guessed right. Let's make it simple for you: from now on, when we’re on school grounds, my name isMs. Harrison.” throwing out the last of her lunch, she said, “The next time you forget that, I’ll give you a week’s worth of detention. Is that understood?”

I hadn’t the foggiest idea how they managed to do it, but Radeon had managed to turn Skyler into a teacher. Looking down at the ground, I said, “Yes, ma'am.”

Sitting back at her desk, Ms. Harrison said, “Okay then; now that that’s settled, what did you want to talk to me about?”

“I was simply wondering if you’d like to come over for dinner; that way you can tell my Mom about Radeon and their trials.”

“Well, the school district frowns on that sort of thing; but they should be fine so long as I don’t make a habit of it.” Looking at me, she said, “Besides; it sounds better than a frozen TV dinner or some fattening burger from the drive-thru. What time should I come over?”

“How does 6:00 sound?”
1111111.title: Reprimanded by the Teacher

11111111.content:
I spent the rest of the school day unable to get Ms. Harrison’s big revelation out of my mind. Everything I saw her doing, from lecturing a student for cheating on his test to chatting with a female colleague in the hall, just served to reinforce the fact that she wasn’t the same person I’d known since Middle School.

I came up with a plan for once school ended; I’d go to Skyler’s house to talk with his parents. Assuming they knew the truth, I’m sure they’d make up some lie about where he was. Once the final bell rang, I left the school and started walking the mile to Skyler’s house.

I barely made it 15 feet before Kelsey ran up to me, “Where are you going, Kev?”

“I’m going over to Skyler Graufman’s house; not sure why, but he’s been on my mind a lot today.” Pausing, I said, “Don’t worry, I’ll be home before Mom.”

The walk to Skyler’s house was quiet. As luck would have it, I arrived there just as Mrs. Graufman pulled into the driveway. A big smile on her face, she said, “Well if it isn’t Kevin Scott; I haven’t seen you in ages.”

Skyler and I used to be good friends in Middle School; we started to part ways Freshman year, but I still looked out for him. Walking up to her, I said, “Hey Mrs. G.”

Stepping out of her car, she said, “What brings you here on this fine afternoon?”

Here goes nothing. Smiling, I said, “I was thinking about Skyler today; and thought I’d come by to see how he was doing.”

I could tell at once that I’d caught her off guard, but she recovered quickly. “He’s adjusting pretty well to his new school; he’s made some friends and started to lose some of the weight.” Pausing, she said, “I doubt you’d recognize him if you saw him on the street.”

You’d be right about that. “Well tell him I came by and said hi; some of us still miss him.”

“I’ll be sure to tell him when he comes home.” Looking at me, she said, “Have a good rest of your day, Kevin.”
11111111.title: After School

111111111.content:
Kelsey and I were outside shooting some hoops when Mom came home from work. I told her about Ms. Harrison as the three of us carried groceries into the house. Mom, of course, jumped to conclusions and said, “Perhaps you could explain why one of your teachers is coming for dinner? You’re not in trouble, are you?”

Kelsey started giggling at the absurdity of Mom’s question; everyone knew that I wasn’t a troublemaker. “Seriously Mom? When have I ever done anything to warrant a visit from a teacher?”

“Sorry sweetie, you’re right.” Sighing as she hefted a particularly heavy bag onto the counter, Mom said, “I’ve had a rough day; and now I’ve to figure a way to make enough sloppy joes for four people instead of three.”

After that, while Kelsey remained in the kitchen to discuss something else, I went back outside. I was back to shooting hoops when I heard a car pulling into the driveway. I turned to find an older model, probably 2014, Honda Civic stop next to my Mom’s car. Seeing Ms. Harrison in through the windshield, I started walking towards her; only stopping when I saw her glaring on me.

Cautiously approaching her car as she stepped out, I said, “H-Hi Ms. Harrison.”

Struggling to remain calm, Ms. Harrison said, “You’ve got some nerve, Kevin. You mind telling me what you were thinking, going to visit my parents?”

I suppose I shouldn’t have been surprised to learn that Mrs. Graufman had called her son, “I was just curious; I wanted to know if they were in on it.”

“Of course, they’re in on it, Kevin; I needed their consent.” Sighing, she said, “Do you realize how close you came to ruining everything? If word got out about who I really was; then Radeon wouldn’t change me back, and I wouldn’t get that $75000.”

“Sorry, Ms. Harrison; it won’t happen again.”

Ms. Harrison suddenly changed her tone; talking like her old self instead of the teacher she’d become. “Seriously man, you’ve been acting strange all day. What’s wrong?”

“You’re seriously asking me what’s wrong?” Taken aback by the sudden tonal change, I said, “One of my oldest friends, who I thought moved away months ago, has been living as an older woman the whole time.”

“I guess I can understand that; I mean, I’d probably act the same way in your position.” Running a hand through her hair, Ms. Harrison said, “I suppose there’s one good thing to come from this; now that you know the truth, maybe you’ll focus less on me and more on your work.”

Mom walked out of the garage before I could respond. “Colleen, I thought that was you.”

With everything else I’d learned today; I wasn’t all that surprised to learn Mom knew Ms. Harrison. I watched as the two of them hugged as if they were old friends. “You two know each other?”

“That’s right, Kevin; your mother and I are in the same spin class at the gym.” Turning back to Mom, placing a hand around her waist, she said, “Which reminds me; we all missed you at the last class, Abby.”

“Sorry about that; I twisted my ankle at work.” a smile on her face, Mom said, “Why don’t we all come inside; dinner should be ready in about 20 minutes.”

Turning back to her car, Ms. Harrison grabbed her purse and a bottle of wine. Handing it to Mom, she said, “I hope you don’t mind I brought some wine.”

“Oh, how thoughtful.” Smiling as she looked at the bottle, Mom said, “This is my favorite.”
111111111.title: Back Home

1111111111.content:
I decided to go wash my hands for dinner while Ms. Harrison carried on a conversation with Mom and Kelsey in the kitchen. I couldn’t really hear it from the bathroom; but what little I heard told me it wasn’t a conversation I’d find interesting. The fact that Ms. Harrison was willingly taking part in a discussion about shoes was another sign of how much she’d changed.

I entered the kitchen just in time to hear Mom ask Ms. Harrison why she was here. Looking at her while opening the bottle of wine, Mom said, “Not that I don’t enjoy seeing you, Colleen; but it’s not every day my children’s teacher comes over for dinner.”

“It’s nothing nefarious, I can assure you.” Pouring herself a glass, Ms. Harrison said, “Kevin had told me about your financial troubles, and I told him of a possible solution.”

Mom turned to me with an annoyed expression on her face, “I don’t know what surprises me more; that you know about that, or that you told your teacher about it.”

Stepping in before I could, Kelsey said, “I told him about it, Mom; I overheard you talking to Aunt Carrie last night.”

“Well, that explains the first part; but not why your brother told Colleen about it.”

Once again, I was interrupted before I could speak; this time by Ms. Harrison, “I’m afraid that’s my fault, Abby; from day one I’ve told my students they can come to me with whatever is bothering them.”

“That’s a wonderful sentiment, Colleen; but surely you agree there should be boundaries.”

“I agree completely; there are certain boundaries that students and teachers should never cross.” Focusing on me, Ms. Harrison said, “One of them should be not visiting a teacher’s parents.”

I winced as everyone focused on me; with Mom saying, “Is there something I should know about, Kevin?”

Feeling two inches tall, I said, “I may have paid a visit to Ms. Harrison’s parents after school.”

“Geez Kevin, you told me you were going to stop by Skyler's house.” In shock, Kelsey said, “I know you’ve got a thing for Ms. Harrison, but visiting her parents borders on stalking.”

Ms. Harrison and I both blushed at the mention of my crush on her. Trying her best to maintain her composure, Mom said, “What were you thinking, young man?”

I was stumped as I struggled to think of a logical explanation. After all it wasn’t like I could tell the truth, because neither Kelsey nor Mom knew the truth about who Ms. Harrison really was. “I-I just wanted to ask her something about school; I-I didn’t think that would be a problem.”

While Ms. Harrison and Kelsey bought my answer, it looked like Mom didn’t. Thankfully Ms. Harrison stepped in before Mom could yell at me, “Perhaps next time you should email me with questions, Kevin.”

“Yes ma’am.”

I went upstairs to my room for a few minutes to compose myself. After taking a few deep breaths, I stepped out of my room and bumped into Ms. Harrison. Chuckling, she did, “You should really watch where you’re going, Kevin.”

Looking at her, I said, “What do you want?”

“First off, that’s no way to talk to a teacher.” Stepping into my room, she said, “Second, I wanted to see how you’re doing.”

Following her back into my room, I said, “Not well; considering everyone ganged up on me down there.” Looking at her, I said, “All this could have been avoided had you come clean about who you really are.”

Under her breath, Ms. Harrison said, “If only it were that easy.”

“What’s that supposed to mean?”

Taking a deep breath Ms. Harrison looked at me and said, “The longer I’m like this; the more I’m inclined to act like a woman. I’m not sure how much of Skyler’s left.”

I was about to respond when Kelsey yelled from downstairs, “Dinner!”

***************


The dinner conversation was pretty straight forward; as the four of us talked about things going on in our lives. Between bites of her sandwich, Ms. Harrison asked Mom, “So Abby, how are things going with Leslie?”

Mom looked as though that wasn’t something she wanted to talk about, “They’re going fine; in fact, we’re getting together for dinner tomorrow.”

Kelsey and I shared a look with each other, we had no idea what was going on. Turning to Mom, Kelsey said, “Whose Leslie, Mom?”

“Leslie is…someone I dated in college before I met your father.” Sighing, Mom said, “If it hadn’t been for me getting scared, I would’ve married Leslie instead of your father.”

“So, are you two getting back together?”

“We’re not sure. Right now we’re just testing the waters a bit.” Turning her attention to Ms. Harrison, Mom said, “How are things between you and Mason?”

I turned to Ms. Harrison because I really wanted to hear about this. “He’s a really sweet guy outside of work; nothing like that macho tough guy he pretends to be during the day.” Wiping some sloppy joe from her face, she continued, “We actually took a pretty big step last weekend; we spent the night together.”

In a day full of surprises that was the last thing I’d expected to hear. Spitting out a mouthful of sloppy joe, all over Kelsey, I said, “You slept with Coach Jacobs!?”

Mom glared at me while Kelsey worked to wipe my dinner off her face. “Kevin, what did we just finish talking about?”

I was about to speak until Ms. Harrison stopped me, “Don’t be too hard on him, Abby; I’m afraid there’s something you need to know.” Sighing, she continued, “It’s actually related to why I’m here.”

Kelsey and Mom exchanged confused looks with each other. Looking at Ms. Harrison, Mom said, “What’s going on, Colleen?”

“I haven’t always been Colleen Harrison; in fact, she didn’t exist until four months ago.” Taking a deep breath, Ms. Harrison said, “I used to be Skyler Graufman.”

I don’t know what I expected to happen, but I know Mom fainting wasn’t it.
1111111111.title: Awkward Dinner Conversation

11111111111.content:
It took some doing, but I got Mom to the couch with some help from Ms. Harrison and Kelsey. After making sure she was comfortable, Kelsey turned her attention to Ms. Harrison: the same look on her face that I had after I’d learned the truth. “Skyler, you...you've had some work done.”

“Trust me; that’s putting it mildly.” Turning to look at Kelsey, Ms. Harrison said, “And not that you’d make a habit of it; but my
name is Colleen now.”

I stood back by the door as Kelsey and Ms. Harrison continued their conversation, “So that advice you gave me last week?”

“Don’t worry Kelsey; my advice was on the level. I simply took what I’d experienced in that area and put it together with what I’ve heard your female classmates saying.” Getting to her feet, Ms. Harrison said, “Let’s table that discussion till later; right now, we should make sure your mother is alright.”

Heading off towards the bathroom, Kelsey said, “We’ve got some smelling salts in the first aid kit; but I need Kevin’s help reaching it.”

I followed Kelsey down the hall to the bathroom as Ms. Harrison stayed with Mom. Closing the door as I walked inside, I said,
“You don’t need my help with the first aid kit; what’s going on?”

Looking at me, Kelsey said, “How long have you known the truth about Skyler…I mean, Ms. Harrison?”

“I only found out this morning before school.” Leaning against the counter, I said, “She made me promise to keep it a secret, which is why I didn’t tell you at lunch.”

“I-I can’t believe this; Skyler Graufman was a chubby kid who followed you around like a lost puppy.” Pacing around the tiny bathroom, Kelsey said, “Ms. Harrison’s a woman; and an attractive one at that.”

“First; Skyler didn’t follow me around like a lost puppy, we were friends.” Wiping some stray sauce from my mouth, I said, “Second; you think she’s attractive?”

“Well yeah; but not in the same way you do.” Seeing the perplexed look on my face, Kelsey said, “What? A straight woman can’t think another woman is attractive?”

I wanted to delve deeper into that but decided to save it for later. “We should probably get back to the family room, Ms. Harrison’s probably wondering where we are.” Watching as Kelsey retrieved the first aid kit, I said, “Just out of curiosity; what advice did she give you?”

The look on her face told me it was something Kelsey wasn’t really interested in discussing. “I was having a particularly bad menstrual cycle, and she gave me advice on how to deal with it.”

With the first aid kit in hand, Kelsey and I returned to the family room; only to find that Mom was conscious and chatting with Ms. Harrison on the couch.
11111111111.title: Dealing with Mom

111111111111.content:
I sat on the couch as Ms. Harrison explained everything to Kelsey and Mom; the shocked expressions on their faces reminiscent of my own earlier today. “It all started a few weeks before the end of school last year; I was feeling particularly low after an encounter with a senior who enjoyed tormenting me.” With a sigh, she continued, “Rather than go directly home after school, I decided to go to the park; where, I’m sorry to say, I considered ending it all.”

Kelsey and Mom gasped; with Mom reaching out for Ms. Harrison’s hand, “What happened next?”

“This kind woman in a business suit approached me and asked what the problem was.” Wiping a stray tear from her face, Ms. Harrison said, “Once I was finished telling my story; she told me who she worked for and explained that they could help me.”

As the rest of us focused our attention on her, Ms. Harrison continued, “The woman drove me home and told my parents what she’d told me. They were skeptical at first, until she showed them a video from one of their studies. Seeing a paralyzed former College football player turned into a young girl was enough to convince them.” Taking a breath, Ms. Harrison said, “My parents and I paid a visit to Radeon’s offices the next day; the study they wanted me to join focused on seeing how people would cope if everything about them was changed.”

Nodding as she processed the information, Mom said, “So they turned you into a grown woman?”

“That’s right, I spend one year as a woman; at which time they’ll change me back and pay me $75,000.” Smiling, Ms. Harrison said, “Let me tell you; having spent the last four months as a woman, I’ll have no problem finding a girlfriend once this is over with.”

Briefly turning to look at me, a bemused expression on her face, Kelsey turned back to Ms. Harrison and said, “There’s one thing I can’t quite figure out about all this.” Taking a breath, she said, “Are you dating Coach Jacobs because of the study, or something else?”

Mom spoke up before Ms. Harrison could answer, “I’d love to know that answer as well; because it would certainly explain your hesitation to date him.”

“No; getting involved with Mason isn’t part of the study, it was just a pleasant surprise.” Sighing, Ms. Harrison said, “When this all started, I wasn’t sure I wanted to date men; and Mason certainly wasn’t high on my list. I mean the man would bully and torment me every chance he got.”

Looking at Ms. Harrison, I said, “Then how did you two start dating?”

“It was a few weeks into the school year; I was leaving an afterschool faculty meeting when he approached me. Considering the way that he treated me before, it came as a surprise to see he was nervous about approaching me. He asked if I’d be interested in going out for drinks some time; I was stunned to say the least but managed to say yes.” Gesturing to Mom, she said, “I’d become friendly with your mother by that point as we always wound up on adjacent bikes. I explained to her that Mason had asked me out; but that I was thinking of backing out because I’d heard stories about him from my students.”

Taking over for Ms. Harrison, Mom said, “I told her to give him a chance; that teenagers always have stories about teacher’s they don’t like.”

“I never thought I’d say this; but I’m glad I gave Mason a chance.” Blushing, Ms. Harrison said, “I know he’s all tough and macho at school; but that’s all an act. He’s one of the sweetest men I’ve ever met; not only to me, but his little girl as well.”

Thrown off guard by how Ms. Harrison was talking about Coach Jacobs, I said, “I’ll take your word on that, Ms. Harrison.”

After a few more minutes discussing Ms. Harrison’s relationship with Coach Jacobs, Mom said, “I enjoy talking about relationships as much as the next woman; but perhaps you should get to the point, Colleen.”

“Sorry; I sometimes lose focus when talking about Mason.” Blushing, Ms. Harrison said, “Regardless; if Radeon is willing to pay me what they’re offering, perhaps they can do the same for you guys as well.”

Looking from Mom to me, Kelsey said, “I suppose the question now should be which one of us applies for one of the studies.”

Ms. Harrison waited patiently while we all pondered the answer to that question. Pointing to Mom, I said, “Mom’s the most logical choice; I mean she’s over 18, so she doesn’t need parental permission.”

“Normally I’d agree with you, Kevin; but my relationship with Leslie is still developing, I don’t want to jeopardize that.” Sighing, she said, “Normally I wouldn’t condone something like this; but I think it has to be one of you.”

Kelsey and I looked at each other for a moment; silent until I said, “Should we do rock paper scissors?”

“I guess that’s the only option we have; best out of 3, or best out of 5?”

“Considering the seriousness of the decision, I think we should do best out of 5.”

As Kelsey and I got situated, Ms. Harrison said, “Whichever one of you it ends up being; just know that I’ll provide whatever support I can.”

It took less than two minutes for the decision to me; Kelsey took an early lead, but I quickly caught up and became the victor. Once the decision was made, Mom looked me and said, “I’ll contact Radeon tomorrow to set up an interview.” Hugging me, Mom said, “I appreciate that you’re willing to make this sacrifice, Kevin.”

Hugging Mom back, I said, “Anything for you, Mom.”

Our hug was interrupted by a notification from someone’s phone; I didn’t know who’s it was until I saw Ms. Harrison reach for her purse. “I should probably get going; Mason wants me to spend the night.”

After saying their goodbyes, Mom and Kelsey went to start cleaning the dishes. I followed Ms. Harrison outside to ask her a question that’d been weighing on my mind all day. Running up to her as she got into her car, I said, “Skyl...Ms. Harrison, there’s one more thing I wanted to ask you.”

Sitting in her car, Ms. Harrison said, “What is it, Kevin?”

Rubbing my arm, I said, “What’s it like sleeping with a guy?”

“I thought we talked about boundaries you shouldn’t cross.”

I looked at her, unsure if I was doing the right thing, “I know; that’s why I’m asking my friend and not my teacher.”

“Well in that case, I suppose I could let it slide just this once.” Turning to me, she said, “It’s the most amazing experience in the world; and not just because of the multiple orgasms or the fact that it’s with someone I care deeply for. Who knows; there could be a time in the not-so-distant future where you can find out for yourself.”

Leaning forward, I hugged Ms. Harrison and said, “Thanks for everything, Ms. H.”
111111111111.title: Talking in the Family Room

1111111111111.content:
Almost an hour had passed since Ms. Harrison left, and I was upstairs in my room playing video games. I was making my second attempt to complete a particularly tough quest in Fallout 76 when there was a knock at my door. Momentarily distracted, I looked back to find that I’d died.

Reaching over to turn off the console, I thought to myself, At least I didn’t have any loot to retrieve. Getting up from the bed, I opened the door to find Kelsey standing there. “Hey sis, what’s up?”

Walking into my room, Kelsey took a seat on my bed, “I’m still trying to wrap my head around everything.” Looking at me, she said, “I know she explained everything, but I still can’t believe that Ms. Harrison is really Skyler.”

“Tell me about it; it’s disturbing when you learn the teacher you’ve got a crush on used to be one your classmates.” Joining her on the bed, I said, “For what it’s worth, she seems happy with her life right now.”

“Yeah, that makes me feel a little better about this.” Looking at me, Kelsey said, “I wonder how you’d fare in her situation.”

I’d been thinking about that as well, “I like to think I’d handle it pretty well; although I’m not sure I’d be so quick to sleep with someone.” Sighing, I turned to Kelsey and said, “Do you think we’re doing the right thing; signing me up for one of these studies?”

“If the money’s as good as Ms. Harrison says, we’d be crazy not to.” Chuckling for a moment, Kelsey said, “I guess it all depends on what kind of study they get you involved in.”

“How would you feel if they decided to turn me into a woman?”

I saw a brief smile flash across Kelsey’s face as she pondered my question, “I wouldn’t tease you, if that’s what you mean.”
Looking at me, she said, “I think being a woman would do you some good; like Ms. Harrison said, it’ll give you firsthand knowledge of womankind.”

Chuckling, I said, “I guess that would help; especially when I eventually find myself a girlfriend.”

Leaning against me, Kelsey said, “Whatever happens, I promise I’ll be there for you every step of the way.”

Leaning my head against hers, I said, “I appreciate it, Kelsey.”

Getting up from the bed, a smile on her face, Kelsey said, “Hey, that’s what twins are for; we support each other.”
1111111111111.title: Back Inside

choices: After the Game (Meeting with Brian & Amy)
11111111111111.content:
It was Saturday morning and, as has been the case for the 2 months, that meant another day at the soccer field watching Kelsey and her team playing a game. I don’t want to sound like I hate spending my Saturdays this way, because I don’t – it’s an excuse to get out of the house, plus it helped that some of Kelsey’s teammates were cute.

We had just arrived at the field; while Kelsey made her way to the locker room, Mom and I went to find some seats. On the way, I bumped into Rachel as she was coming out of the bathroom. I could tell she wasn’t thrilled by the idea of sitting out the rest of the season. “Hey Rachel, h-how are you doing?”

“Hey Kevin; I think I’m doing pretty good, all things considered.” Gesturing to her stomach, she said, “So far the worst I’ve had to deal with is the morning sickness.”

I’ll be honest, I was surprised when Kelsey told me Rachel had gotten herself pregnant. She was a straight A student with a full ride to Penn State next Fall. “I apologize if this is inappropriate; but how did it happen?”

Rachel flashed an angry look at me for a moment, “I made a stupid mistake after Homecoming last month; I thought it would be okay if my boyfriend and I had sex.” With a sad chuckle, she continued, “Now he’s left me, and I’ll probably lose my scholarship because it hinged on my ability to play soccer.”

“I’m sorry to hear about your boyfriend; but it sounds like he’s not the type of person you want around your baby.” Trying my best to sound sincere, I said, “But on the bright side, you’ve got Kelsey and the rest of the team for support.”

“I wish I could say that was true; a lot of them are angry with me for jeopardizing our chances at the championship.” Looking at me, she said, “Your sister and one or two others are the only ones who have my back right now.”

Rachel and I spoke for a few more minutes before parting ways. I went to go find my Mom; only to bump into Ms. Harrison and Coach Jacobs over by the concession stand. A quick look also told me Coach Jacobs’ daughter was standing behind him, looking at the menu.

While Ms. Harrison had a smile on her face, Coach Jacobs just stared at me. For a moment, I thought he was going to be a jerk; until he smiled and said, “Hello Mr. Scott; you here to support your sister?”

Taken aback by his kindness, I said, “Y-Yes sir; I think this will be interesting game for them.”

“I’m sure it will be a tough game; but we’ve got a good team this year.” Turning to Ms. Harrison, he gave her a kiss on the cheek said, “I’m going to order us some food; why don’t you find us a seat, okay?”

After reciprocating the kiss, Ms. Harrison and I started walking towards the stairs leading to the bleachers. We barely made it to the stairs before she said, “Would you ask your question already?”

“Huh?”

“Don’t play coy with me, Kevin; I’ve been your friend for years, I know when somethings bugging you.” Stopping just short of the stairs, Ms. Harrison said, “It’s something to do with Mason, isn’t it?”

I was silent for a moment, “Yeah; after last night I wasn’t sure I believed what you said about Coach being a different person outside of school.” Looking at her, I said, “It turns out you were right; he is a much nicer person.”

“Give me some credit, Kevin; do you think I’d date someone who was an asshole?” sighing, Ms. Harrison said, “The way he acts during school is a defense mechanism; after his girlfriend left him and Amber, he didn’t want to risk getting close to anyone again.”

I’d heard rumors about his girlfriend leaving him; but I always thought it was because of his attitude, not the cause of it. “What made him take a chance with you?”

“His friends convinced him to get back out there.”

With a smile on my face, I said, “Now that you mention it, he has seemed happier lately.” Looking at Ms. Harrison, I said, “I guess we have you to thank for that?”

“That’s right.” Smiling, she said, “Now let’s go find some seats before they’re all taken.”
11111111111111.title: At the Soccer Field

111111111111111.content:
It came as no surprise that Kelsey and the team trounced their opponent 8-3. The team headed to one of the local pizza joints for the celebratory meal; with Brian and I tagging along as well.

Brian and I sat in a booth off to one side while the team celebrated their win. Rachel was there as well but I noticed most of the team was giving her the cold shoulder.

I was minding my own business eating a slice of pizza when Brian nudged me. “So when are you going to her how you feel?”

“Huh?”

“Don’t act like you have no idea what I’m talking about.” Grinning, he said, “When are you going to tell Amy about your feelings for her?”

It was an open secret between Brian and me that I’ve had a crush on Amy since the 8th grade. I was hesitant to act on it because I didn’t want to jeopardize my friendship. “I’d hate for things to get weird between us.”

“Oh come on; you two have been best friends for years.” Chuckling, Brian said, “You two getting together would simply be the next step in your friendship.”

“This is all academic anyway; what if she doesn’t feel that way about me?”

Leaning closer, his voice a whisper, Brian said, “I’m not supposed to know this; but I have it on good faith that Amy does feel that way about you.”

I was ecstatic; but Amy and Kelsey walked over before I could say anything. Joining us in the booth, Amy said, “So Kev, Kelsey said you had something you wanted to talk with us about?”

Still on cloud nine from Brian’s news, I was momentarily confused by her statement. “Huh?”

Looking at me, with a serious tone, Kelsey said, “You know; that thing Ms. Harrison told us about?”

“Oh right!” my mind suddenly clear again, I said, “Remember how we told you about my mom’s money problems? Ms. Harrison came over and told us a way we could make a lot of money.”

Brian and Amy looked at each other for a moment before looking back at us. Leaning forward, Amy said, “It’s all above board, right?”

“Seriously Amy? Does Ms. Harrison seem like the type to do something illegal?”

“Well she is dating Coach Jacobs, so who knows what she does.”

Ignoring the jab at Ms. Harrison’s expense, I said, “There’s a company that pays good money to take part in long-term studies.” Pausing, I said, “I heard about them from Skyler a while back; I didn’t give it much thought until Ms. Harrison explained it.”

I went onto explain everything that I’d learned last night, keeping out details that would jeopardize things for Ms. Harrison. When I was finished, I said, “I’m not not what study they’ll put me in, or if they’ll even take me; but whatever happens, I need to know you guys will be there to support me.”

Amy and Brian looked at each other for a moment before turning back to me. “We’ll support you 100%, Kev; that’s what friends are for.”

“Thanks guys.” Smiling, I said, “My mom called to schedule an interview for Monday morning; which means Kelsey and I won’t be at school that day.”

“Be sure to keep us posted, alright?”
111111111111111.title: After the Game

1111111111111111.content:
Kelsey and I had planned to stay at the restaurant longer; until we both got a text from Mom telling us to come home. We knew it wasn’t an emergency, because Mom would’ve called us, but we still rushed home.

It took us some time to walk home; but when we rounded the street corner, we saw an unfamiliar car sitting in the driveway. Looking at Kelsey, I said, “I wonder who that could be?”

Approaching the car, Kelsey said, “There’s a Penn State Alumni sticker in the rear window; I wonder if this is Leslie’s car?”

Looking through the car, I saw nothing to tell me who Leslie was. Turning to Kelsey, I said, “I wonder if Mom wanted us to meet him?”

Heading towards the front door, Kelsey said, “Your guess is as good as mine, Kev; why don’t we go inside and find out.”

Following Kelsey, I used my keys to unlock the front door. Walking inside with Kelsey, I heard Mom say, “You’re worrying over nothing, Leslie; I’m sure my kids will love you.”

I couldn't hear Leslie's response because they were too far away. Before I could say anything, Kelsey said, “Mom? We’re home!”

“We’re in the kitchen; there’s someone I want you to meet.”

Kelsey and I looked at each other anxiously as we walked down the hall into the kitchen. We stopped in our tracks the moment we entered the room. It was surprising enough to learn the truth about Ms. Harrison; but that paled in comparison to the surprise sitting before us. Leslie was a woman.
1111111111111111.title: Returning Home

11111111111111111.content:
To say that Kelsey and I were stunned would be the understatement of the century; and this after hearing one of my childhood friends had become my history teacher. Standing just inside the kitchen, while trying to be respectful, I got a good look at Leslie. She was an attractive red head; with an athletic body that didn’t have an ounce of fat on it.

As Kelsey and I joined them at the table, I looked at Mom and said, “Mom, are you a lesbian?”

“No sweetie, I’m bisexual.” Gesturing to Leslie, Mom said, “I’d had my suspicions throughout High School; but it wasn’t until I met Leslie that I figured out the truth.”

“I’ve always been a sucker for stories like this.” Looking at Mom, Kelsey said, “You have to tell us how the two of you met.”

“It was just about a month into the school year; one of the fraternities was throwing a party.” With a smile on her face, Mom said, “I was already feeling a little tipsy; when I spotted this adorable red head sitting on a nearby couch.”

“So, who made the first move?”

Leslie and Mom both looked at each other; before Leslie said, “Most of that evening was a blur; but I think it was me who made the first move.”

Taking over for Leslie, Mom said, “We wound up back at my dorm, where we spent the night together.” Smiling, Mom said, “A few
days later, Leslie asked me out; and we dated for almost 3 years.”

Looking at Mom and Leslie, Kelsey said, “It sounds like you two were perfect for each other; so why did you break up?”

“I’m afraid that’s my fault.” Leslie sighed as she looked at Mom, “My parents already knew that your mother and I were dating, but your mom hadn’t told her parents yet.”

“We had a nasty fight that culminated in the two of us breaking up.” Looking sad, Mom said, “I started dating your father a few weeks later, and the rest is history.”

Kelsey looked at me before turning to Mom, “So now comes a pretty obvious question; how did you two meet up again?”

Mom and Leslie exchanged looks with each other, before Leslie turned to us and said, “I found your mother on Facebook and we started talking. We met for drinks and it was like no time has passed at all.”

Kelsey and I were smiling, thrilled to see that Mom appeared to be happy. Getting up, the two of us hugged her. Looking at her, Kelsey said, “We’re so happy for you, Mom; whatever happens going forward, you have our support.”

“I’m so relieved to hear you say that.” Hugging us, Mom said, “Because I was hoping the four of us could go out for dinner tonight.”
11111111111111111.title: Meeting Leslie

111111111111111111.content:
Kelsey and I quickly got changed into outfits appropriate for a restaurant; after which the four of us drove to a popular local steak house. Kelsey and I waited until after the orders had been placed to start asking questions. We decided that I would go first.

Looking at Leslie as I drank my soda, I said, “So Leslie, what do you do for a living?”

“I recently started a job as the Events Coordinator for the Halcyon Hotel.” Smiling, she said, “I’m the person people call when they want to book our venue for a wedding reception or another event.”

“That actually sounds fun, I bet you meet a lot of interesting people in that field.”

“Oh, most definitely; in fact, the hotel is hosting a gala fundraiser next Wednesday.” Taking a sip of her wine, Leslie said, “If there’s anyone I can’t stand its socialites and their spoiled wives; everything has to be absolutely perfect for them.”

Waiting until after she’d swallowed some bread, Kelsey said, “I hope this isn’t too personal, Leslie; but did you ever get married?”

While Mom glared at Kelsey, Leslie said, “I came close once; but then I caught her in bed with her male co-worker.” Gesturing to Mom, she said, “That was 3 years ago; your mother is the first person I’ve dated since that happened.”

Looking at Mom, I said, “So you guys are dating now? I thought you were taking things slow?”

Smiling, Mom said, “We are taking things slow, Kevin; which is why we haven’t slept together yet.”

Elbowing me in the stomach, Kelsey said, “Who cares if they’re dating or not, Kev; I’m happy as long as Mom’s happy.”

“You’re right.” Turning to Mom, I said, “Sorry I questioned you, Mom; I’m happy for you.”
111111111111111111.title: Dinner

1111111111111111111.content:
The rest of the weekend passed by without much going on; Kelsey and I spent time with our friends. While Kelsey made sure to get her homework finished for Tuesday, I was on the fence about doing the same. For all I knew I’d end up like Ms. Harrison; so, there was really no point in doing all that homework.

I walked into the kitchen just after 7:00 on Monday morning to find Mom and Kelsey were already there waiting for me. Placing a big plate of eggs, sausage, and bacon in front of me, Mom kissed my forehead and said, “I can’t thank you enough for what you’re doing, Kevin.”

Taking a bite of the bacon, I said, “We don’t even know if they’ll take me, Mom; Ms. Harrison said she had three interviews before they finally took her.”

“I have faith that they’ll take you without hesitation.” Sitting across the table from me, Mom said, “Is there anything you need from me or your sister?”

“Yeah; actually, there is.” Putting my fork down, my voice turning serious, I said, “If I become a woman, I want you guys to promise not to tease me.”

Mom and Kelsey looked at each other for a moment; before finally turning to me. With a sincere look on her face, Kelsey said, “I’ll try my best not to tease you too much.”

I wasn’t really buying Kelsey’s answer, but I knew that Mom would keep her in line. After finishing my breakfast, and engaging in more small talk, I headed upstairs to get dressed. I barely passed the front door when I heard the doorbell ring.

Peaking outside the window, I was surprised to see Amy standing on the front porch. Opening the door, I said, “Amy, what are you doing here? School starts in like 20 minutes.”

“I know; but I wanted to see you before the interview.” Looking at me, Amy said, “I wanted to wish you luck; and give you a little going away present.”

Amy reached over before I could say anything and kissed me on the lips. The kiss was passionate, and I found myself reciprocating it. Eventually, I broke away and looked at Amy, “What was that about?”

Blushing, Amy said, “That’s a taste of what awaits you after the study is over.”

So, Brian had been telling the truth; Amy did have feelings for me. My heart racing, I said, “What if I decided not to go through with the study? Could we start dating now?”

“I suppose we could; but I’ve never known you to back out of anything before.” Kissing me on the cheek, Amy said, “I’ll see you later, Kevin.”

I watched as Amy walked down the driveway and hopped on her bike. I didn’t close the door until she was out of sight. Turning around, I saw Kelsey standing nearby with a smile on her face, “So, Amy finally confessed her feelings for you, huh?”
1111111111111111111.title: The Day of the Interview

11111111111111111111.content:
The conversation during the ride to Radeon was focused entirely on my kiss with Amy. Kelsey was acting as if she’d known it was coming, while Mom was completely shocked. “I didn’t realize the two of you felt that way about each other.”

I tried my best not to think too much about the kiss as Mom parked the car outside of Radeon’s offices in a business park just outside of town. I got a good look at the building as we got out of the car; it looked like pretty much every other office building I’d ever seen. But the building’s appearance belied its true purpose.

Walking next to me as we entered the lobby, Kelsey said, “Do you think they turned Skyler into a woman here, or someplace else?”

I honestly had no idea, so I shrugged and said, “If I had to guess, they probably did it here.” Pausing, I said, “That way they can better control who knows what.”

After checking in, we were directed to a waiting room on the second floor. Once we entered the room, I saw three other people already there; a man probably a few years older than mom, and two younger women. One of the women, a blonde, looked familiar for some reason. The other one, a brunette, seemed more interested in her cellphone than anything else.

Leaning close to my ear, Kelsey said, “I wonder if they’re here for the same reason you are.”

Looking closer at the three of them, I had to wonder if Kelsey was right. The two women were dressed in what I could only imagine were expensive clothes, while the man was wearing shirt and tie. The more I looked at the three of them, the more I started to doubt we were all here for the same reason.

One by one an employee came to take someone away; until the only people left were me, Kelsey, and Mom. Eventually a young, visibly pregnant, woman entered the room. Looking at us, she said, “You must be the Scott family.”

“That’s us.” Standing next to me, Mom said, “My son is interested in taking part in one of your studies.”

“Well then, shall we get things started?” smiling, the woman said, “My name’s Elena; and I’ll be conducting your son’s interview to see if he fits our criteria.”

*********************************


The interview took two hours; with Elena asking everyone questions – about our home life, my social life, etc. A few of the questions got personal; especially when Elena asked how I’d feel if I were to become a woman.

Once the interview was finished, Elena said, “I’ve got everything I need; I’m going to speak with my supervisor to get their opinion.”

No one said a word until after Elena left; but once the door closed, Mom looked at Kelsey and me, “That interview was far more extensive than I suspected; it would have been nice had Colleen warned us about that.”

“Don’t be too hard on her, Mom; Ms. Harrison has probably grown used to keeping certain things close to her chest.”

Another 10 minutes passed before Elena walked in with an older looking gentleman. Taking a seat across from us, the man said, “Hello folks; my name is Alistair Cummings, I’m in charge of recruitment at that facility.”

“It’s nice to meet you, sir.” Getting apprehensive, I said, “Forgive me; but have you come to a decision?”
11111111111111111111.title: At Radeon

111111111111111111111.content:
I didn’t even realize I was holding my breath until I felt that familiar burning sensation in my lungs. Taking a deep breath, I looked at Mr. Cummings and said, “You’re taking me into the program?”

Nodding, a smile on his face, Mr. Cummings said, “That’s right; me and Elena were both impressed by your commitment to your family.” Looking at me, he said, “It’s my hope that you’ll bring that same commitment to whatever study you wind up in.”

After exchanging congratulatory remarks with Kelsey and Mom, I turned back to Mr. Cummings, “Just what kind of study will I end up in?”

“That’s entirely up to you; we’ve got a wide array of potential studies you can take part in.” placing a file on the table in front of me, Mr. Cummings said, “Why don’t you have a look and tell me what one you’d like to take part in. Elena and I will be waiting outside for your answer.”

I waited until after Elena and Mr. Cummings left the room before I grabbed the file. Situating myself so that Mom and Kelsey could read it too, I opened the file and looked at the options before me.

Study #1 (Bridge): Swap lives with another individual; live that person’s life for a period of two years. Upon completion receive $100000.

Study #2 (Identity): Everything about my life is changed; including my age, gender, sexual orientation, etc. Live that way for two years and get $75000.

Study #3 (Mirror Image): Swap a personality trait, or knowledge, with someone else. Live this way for one year and each participant gets $50000.

After everyone had looked over the options and discussed them, I got up to inform Mr. Cummings of my decision. Opening the door, I looked at him and said, “I’d like to go with ______.”
111111111111111111111.title: You're In The Program

1111111111111111111111.content:
Mr. Cummings looked at me, “Are you absolutely certain that is the one you want to take part in?”

“Yeah, I’m sure.” Getting nervous, I said, “Why? Is that a problem?”

“No, it’s not a problem.” Stepping closer to me, Mr. Cummings said, “It’s just that some people find it a little disconcerting to think they’ll be living someone else’s life. I'd hate for you to undergo the procedure only to be disturbed by the thought of being another person."

I hadn’t thought of that way; what if the person I swapped with wasn’t reputable. “It’s not too late for me to change my mind, is it?”

“No, it’s not; but let me remind you, this particular study has the greatest financial payout.”

After thinking it over once more, I decided to ______.
1111111111111111111111.title: Study #1

11111111111111111111111.content:
After reaffirming my decision to stay with Study #1, Mr. Cummings and I walked back into the room where I’d left Kelsey and mom waiting. Mr. Cummings didn’t bother sitting as he looked at us, “Now that Kevin has made his decision, I’m going to take him to meet the others who have chosen this particular study.” Focusing on Mom and Kelsey, he said, “You can return once the procedure is complete.”

Turning to Mr. Cummings as she got up from the table, Mom said, “Is there anything we need to know before we leave?”

“In order to facilitate a proper adjustment, we ask that you treat the person in Kevin’s body as if they were the real Kevin.” Turning me, Mr. Cummings continued, “As part of that, you must also treat your family as if you’ve never met them.”

I was starting to have misgivings again, “Wait, I thought I’d be allowed to remain in contact with my family.”

“You will be allowed to contact them; but not until after your 24-hour adjustment period has ended.” With a forced smile, Mr. Cummings said, “After that you’ll be free to contact them; or you can choose not to if you’re so inclined.”

I gave Mom and Kelsey hugs before following Mr. Cummings down the hall to another section of the building. I started getting nervous as we approached a closed door at the end of a hallway. Looking inside as Mr. Cummings opened the door; I saw the same three people I’d seen in the waiting room.

Whereas before I hadn’t paid much attention to them; this time around I figured I better pay attention since I would soon be living as one of them. Taking one last look at Mr. Cummings before going inside, I said, “So how does this work? Do I get to choose, or do you?”

“We find it makes things more interesting if we make the final decision.” Smiling, Mr. Cummings said, “But don’t worry; I’m sure you’ll have a wonderful time as any one of these people.”

With that, Mr. Cummings gave me a gentle shove inside the room before closing the door. Walking over to the table, I made sure to get a good look at each of them.

The man appeared to be in his mid-thirties; he wasn’t in terrible shape, but he could work out more.

Looking at the blonde, I couldn’t shake the feeling that I knew her from somewhere. Setting that thought aside for the moment, I noticed that she was extremely attractive; wearing an outfit that showed off her figure. If I had to guess she was only a few years older than me

Getting to the brunette, judging by her appearance, I figured that she was in her late twenties. Dressed in a business suit, she had an air of professionalism about her.

None of us said a word as the minutes ticked by; no doubt focusing on the others in the room. Eventually the door opened, and Elena gestured for me to follow her into the hallway. Once outside, she looked at me and said, “I’m sure you’re no doubt interested in who we’ve picked for you.”

“Very interested.” Looking back into the room, I said, “But before you tell me; what’s going to happen to the two I don’t swap with?”

“Don’t worry; none of you will leave unswapped.” Removing a piece of paper from her pocket, Elena said, “As for you; we’ve decided to swap you with ______.”
11111111111111111111111.title: I'll Stay with Study #1

111111111111111111111111.content:
Elena took David and me through the building to a private, well-decorated room for us to talk. Standing in the doorway as we took our seats, she said, “Feel free to take as long as you want; I’ll come get you when you’re ready.”

Elena left after we both acknowledged her; leaving me alone in a room with a man old enough to be my father. Neither of us said a word for a few minutes as we looked each other over; it was weird focusing so much on what an older man looked like, but I reminded myself it was going to be my body soon enough.

He was tall, probably around 5’10”, with a muscular build – my guess is that he worked out regularly. After a few minutes of looking each other over, David said, “So Kevin, why don’t you tell me about yourself?”

“There’s really not much to tell; I’m 15 years old, I live with my Mom and twin sister. I’m on the school swim team; and my best friends are Amy Williams and Brian Sullivan. I’ve had a crush on Amy since the 7th grade and, after an incident this morning, I’m fairly certain she feels the same way.”

“I can’t help but notice you didn’t mention your father. Is he still alive?”

Looking down for a moment, I said, “I honestly have no idea; he abandoned us when I was 8 years old.”

“That’s terrible; anyone who does that doesn’t deserve to have a family.” Looking at me, David said, “It must’ve been tough on your mother; having to raise two children on her own.”

“That’s kind of why I’m here; to help her financially.” Sitting back in the chair, I said, “Why don’t you tell me a little about yourself?”

“Well; I have a son that’s your age, we live about 20 minutes from here. My wife died while giving birth to our second child, so it’s just been me and Andrew for the last 7 years.” Sighing, David said, “He’s actually the reason I’m doing this; he’s grown distant the last few years. I’ll be honest, I was hoping they’d pair us together.”

“Really?”

“The moment I saw you walk into that room; I realized the best way to repair my relationship with Andrew would be to understand what it’s like to be a modern teenager.” Pausing, David said, “After two years living your life, I’ll have no problem bonding with my son.”

“And I guess spending two years as a father will help me in the future.”
111111111111111111111111.title: David (The Man)

111111111111111111111112.content:
I can’t explain why, but I had hoped I would end up swapping with Sienna. Don’t get me wrong; I wouldn’t have had a problem regardless of who I swapped with. It was just that Sienna was closer in age to me than the others, so adapting to her life wouldn’t be difficult.

Elena led the two of us to another section of the building, while another employee took the other two. Once we were seated, Elena left the room to give us some privacy. Sienna and I spent the first few minutes looking each other over; I still couldn’t shake the feeling that I knew her from somewhere.

Sienna must’ve read my mind; looking at me, with a British accent, she said, “You recognize me from somewhere, don’t you? You just can’t quite figure out where?”

“Yeah; and it’s been bugging me since I first saw you.” Pausing, I said, “Where do I know you from?”

Grabbing her phone, Sienna pulled up a photo before handed it off to me. “Perhaps this will jog your memory.”

Looking at Sienna’s phone, I saw a photo of her smiling as she stood in front of a Victoria’s Secret display – one that had a photo of her in nothing but pants and a bra. My jaw dropping, I said, “You’re a model?”

“That’s right; and a pretty good one, I might add.”

Once I got over my shock, Sienna and I got down to the business of discussing our respective lives. Although she was from London, Sienna currently lived in New York City. She’s been dating a photographer for almost a year.

The more I talked to her, the more confused I got as to why she was doing this. “Forgive me for asking; but why would you give up such an amazing career for something like this? You’d probably make $100000 in no time flat.”

“Well of course I would; I make almost $8000 a week.” Chuckling, Sienna said, “But to answer your question; I want to take a break from my life, try something that’s less public.”

“I suppose I can’t blame you for wanting to take a break.”

“Now let me ask you something, Kevin.” Leaning closer, Sienna said, “Are you sure you can spend the next two years living my life? You’ll be putting yourself in situations where a lot of your classmates will see you wearing practically nothing at all.”

I hadn’t thought of that until Sienna mentioned it; she was one of the models that my male classmates were obsessed with. Hell, even Brian had a picture of her in his locker. Looking at Sienna, I said, “I’m sure it’ll take some getting used to; but I think I can handle it.”
111111111111111111111112.title: Sienna (The Blonde)

111111111111111111111113.content:
Ever since I learned the truth about Ms. Harrison, I’d imagined what it would be like living as a woman. Of course, my hope was to be in the same situation as Ms. Harrison; but I didn’t mind the idea of switching lives with a woman either.

Looking through the tiny window, I focused on Amelia. Turning back to Elena, I said, “What can you tell me about her?”

“You’ll get the chance to speak with her soon enough; but I can tell you this: she’s recently divorced and has a fair amount of money.”

“So, I’ll be a rich and attractive divorce for two years?” With a smile on my face, I said, “I suppose there are worse ways to make $100000.”

Placing a caring hand on my shoulder, Elena said, “Why don’t we get Elena; I’ll bring you both somewhere to talk in private.”

I waited outside as Elena grabbed Amelia from the room. I noticed her eying me intently as we walked down the hall; no doubt sizing up her new body.

As we stopped outside another closed door, I looked at Elena, "How exactly are you going to turn me into Amelia, and vice versa? Advanced plastic surgery?"

It seemed that Amelia had the same question; focusing on Elena as the latter said, "We've created a device that can convert a person's consciousness to into raw data." opening the door, she continued, "After that, we simply transfer that data to your new body."
111111111111111111111113.title: Amelia (The Brunette)

1111111111111111111111131.content:
Elena opened the door to let Amelia and I inside the room, which seemed much nicer than the room I’d had my interview in. It reminded me a sitting room in an older house, with comfortable chairs and a fake fireplace.

Taking a seat across from Amelia, I looked at her and said, “So, uh, how do you want to do this?”

Crossing her legs as she sat down, Amelia said, “I was hoping you’d know; I’ve never done anything like this before.”

Chuckling, I said, “I didn’t even know this was possible until a few days ago.” Leaning back in the chair, I continued, “I suppose the most logical thing to do would be to say a little about ourselves.”

“That makes sense to me; after all, we’re each going to be living someone else’s life for two years.” Leaning forward, Amelia said, “Why don’t you get the ball rolling.”

I couldn’t help but stare at Amelia’s chest as she looked at me. I guess that’s going to be my chest soon enough. I’d seen enough photos of celebrity women to know when someone had work done. “I hope this isn’t inappropriate of me; but did you have work done?”

“You have a keen eye, Kevin.” Leaning back in the chair, Amelia said, “I suffered from image issues throughout High School; so, the summer before I started college, I convinced my parents to let me get some work done. I went from a B to a D and waved goodbye to my body image issues.”

“Well now that that’s out of the way; perhaps we should get to know each other a bit more.”

Amelia and I took turns answering questions for the next hour. I told her all about my family life, my friends, and the swim team.
She told me how her ex-husband made her quit a good paying job so he could have a trophy wife; only to get caught having an affair with his secretary. “I hope I haven’t scared you off, Kevin; but I thought you deserved the truth.”

“I appreciate it, Amelia; I’d rather be a rich divorcee than married to a cheating scumbag.” Looking at her, I said, “While we’re on the subject, I hope you don’t mind getting involved with a teenage girl.”

“Sweetie, you forget I used to be a teenage girl.” Smiling, Amelia said, “Don’t be surprised if you find yourself with big shoes to fill once the study is finished.”

I was about to respond when there was a knock at the door, followed by Elena poking her head inside. “We’re just finishing up with David and Sienna; you two won’t have to wait much longer.”
1111111111111111111111131.title: Getting to Know Amelia

11111111111111111111111311.content:
It wasn’t long before Amelia and I were brought into a small room; empty except for two inclined tables connected to a machine in the middle. Stepping further into the room, I could see another room behind some glass.

A man in a lab coat stepped out of the other room; gesturing to the tables, he said, “If you two will please have a seat, we’ll get started.” Kindly nodding as we walked past, he said, “It won’t be long now before you’re off living your new lives.”

Once Amelia and I were situated on the tables, I looked at her and said, “I have to admit, I’m kind of nervous about this whole thing.” With a nervous chuckle, I said, “What if it turns out that I don’t like being a woman?”

“You need to relax, Kevin; it’s only going to be for two years.” Smiling, Amelia said, “After that you’ll have your male body back; as well as firsthand knowledge of what it’s like being a woman.”

Chuckling again, I said, “I suppose you’re right.”

After placing metal helmets on our heads, the lab tech joined Elena in the other room. Speaking into a microphone, he said, “You’re both going to lose consciousness during the procedure; but you needn’t worry about that. You’ll be in your new bodies before you know it.”

Amelia and I were looking at each other as the lab tech pressed a button; the smile on her face the last thing I saw before losing consciousness.

*******************************


I had no way of knowing how much time had passed since the procedure. The only thing I was aware of as I regained consciousness was the fact my body felt different...and a little achy. I was little taken back when I heard a feminine groan escape my lips.

Opening my eyes, I found myself in a spartan looking hospital room. Looking down, I realized the procedure had worked when I saw my new cleavage showing through the top I was wearing.
11111111111111111111111311.title: The Procedure

111111111111111111111113111.content:
I became increasingly aware of a plethora of new sensations as my shock wore off. One of those sensations came as I moved to sit on the edge of the bed – a sharp pain in my back.

I was oblivious to the cause until my gaze turned to my chest. My well-endowed chest was causing unwanted strain on my back.

Rubbing a hand on my back, I said to myself, “Now I know why Aunt Carrie had breast reduction surgery. This is going to take some getting used to.”

The sound of my new voice gave me pause. Of courseI knew what it sounded like; because I’d spent an hour listening to Amelia talk. What gave me pause was the realization that I was the one speaking.

Hopping off the bed, I quickly found myself faced with another big change – my center of gravity was off. I barely took one step before falling forward; slamming my chest against the seat cushion in front of me.

Massaging my sore chest, I said, “God, I hope I didn’t bite off more than I could chew.” Getting to my feet again, I said, “I wonder how long it took Skyler to adjust to all this.”

Once I was steady of my feet once more, I took a few cautious steps. I stumbled a few times before figuring out the best way to do things – lean back ever so slightly to change my center of gravity.

I spent the next few minutes walking around the room, enjoying the swaying of my hips with each step. Feeling tired, I sat down in the chair just as the door opened and Elena walked inside.

“How are you feeling, Amelia?” Closing the door, she said, “I trust you’re adjusting?”

Part of me wanted to ask why she called me Amelia; until I remembered that’s who I was now. Looking at her, I said, “I wasn’t prepared for all the back strain my chest would cause.” Moving around in the seat, I said, “Plus my center of gravity is all off; I fell chest-first into the chair when I took my first steps.”

“Welcome to life as a woman, Amelia; just be thankful you’re not dealing with a pregnancy.” Placing a hand on her belly. Elena said, “I’ve been pregnant for 6 months now, and I still find myself bumping into things with my belly.”

Eying Elena’s belly, I said, “I’ll have to take your word on that; I have no intention of getting pregnant during this study.”

“You say that now; but what happens after you’ve adjusted to your new body? You might find yourself feeling the call of motherhood.”

Shaking my head, I said, “And leave the real Amelia with a baby she has no connection too? I don’t think I could do that to her.”

“I suppose that’s fair.” Smiling, Elena said, “Your ride to the hotel is here; I can walk you out if you’re ready.”

Eying the pair of heels by the bed, I said, “I’ve never walked in heels before, so I’m going to need your help with that.” Getting to my feet, I said, “Which reminds me; what am I going to do about driving?”

“You don’t have to worry about that.” Watching as I slipped into the heels, Elena said, “While you have a valid driver’s license, you prefer to have other people drive you around.”

Hearing that, I looked at Elena and said, “Be honest with me; does the real Amelia seem as though she’s spoiled?”

“This stays between you and me; but yes, I think she’s a little spoiled.” Looking at me, Elena said, “Perhaps being in your body, living your life, will have a positive effect on her.”

Slowly standing in the heels, my legs wobbling a bit, I said, “If that’s true, I just hope being her doesn’t have a negative effect on me.”

“It shouldn’t; so long as you don’t let it. It’s all about willpower.” Reaching out with her hand, Elena said, “Take my hand; I’ll support you down the hall until you get the hang of heels.”
111111111111111111111113111.title: Getting Acclimated

1111111111111111111111131111.content:
The first time I ever walked in heels wasn’t pretty; I was all sorts of wobbly. I would have fallen and twisted my ankle had it not been for Elena’s help. We took things slow as the two of us walked through the halls towards the main entrance.

I was doing much better once we got to the stairs leading down to the first floor.
